mcp.helpLine1: enables Agents to call external tools and services via a standardized protocol.
mcp.helpLine2: Supports three transports: stdio (local process), Streamable HTTP (remote service), and SSE (legacy MCP servers).
mcp.helpLine3: Built-in configs are in the mcps/ directory. User/AI-added configs are saved in data/mcp/servers/, one subdirectory per server.

mcp.headersHint: One per line, format KEY=VALUE. Supports ${VAR} variable substitution (from .env file).

config.ctxBoundaryRatioHelp: Compression ratio for old topic after topic switch (0.05~0.5)
config.ctxMinRecentTurnsHelp: Minimum recent conversation groups to keep during compression (4~20)
config.ctxEnableToolCompressionHelp: Independently compress oversized tool results via LLM
config.ctxLargeToolThresholdHelp: Compress tool results exceeding this token count

agreement.title: Risk Acknowledgment
agreement.subtitle: Please read the following carefully before proceeding
agreement.content:
Synapse is an AI Agent software powered by Large Language Models (LLMs). Before using it, you need to understand and accept the following:

1. Behavior Is Not Fully Predictable
The AI Agent's behavior is driven by underlying LLMs, which produce probabilistic and non-deterministic outputs. Even with identical inputs, the Agent may produce different results, including but not limited to: unexpected file operations, unintended messages, and unintended tool invocations.

2. Supervision Is Required
You are responsible for supervising the AI Agent's actions at all times. For tool calls that require approval (such as file deletion, system command execution, message sending, etc.), review the operation details carefully before approving. It is strongly recommended NOT to enable auto-confirm mode without active supervision.

3. Potential Risks
The AI Agent may cause: data loss or corruption (e.g., accidental file deletion, overwriting important data), sending inappropriate messages (e.g., incorrect content through IM channels), executing dangerous system commands, generating unexpected API calls and costs, and other unforeseen side effects.

4. Disclaimer of Liability
Synapse is an open-source software project provided "AS IS" without warranty of any kind, express or implied. The project maintainers and contributors shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising from the use of this software. You assume all risks associated with using this software.

5. Data Security
Your conversation content, configuration data, and tool call records may be sent to third-party LLM service providers. Do not share sensitive personal information, passwords, keys, or other confidential data in conversations unless you fully understand and accept the associated risks.

claudeCode.title: Developer CLI setup
claudeCode.subtitle: Detect any one of: Claude Code, Cursor CLI, or OpenCode. Claude / OpenCode can be installed from this bundle with no download; install Cursor yourself first and ensure `cursor` is on PATH.
claudeCode.toolsListDesc: Claude Code and OpenCode use files shipped in this installer; Cursor is not installed from here.
claudeCode.claudeInstallHint: Click Install to copy the bundled Claude Code into your workspace and update PATH (Windows, offline).
claudeCode.cursorInstallHint: Install the Cursor desktop app from the official site, then run “Install cursor command in PATH” inside Cursor (or add `cursor` to PATH yourself). This wizard will not download it.
claudeCode.opencodeInstallHint: Click Install to extract the bundled OpenCode zip into your workspace and update PATH (Windows x64, offline).
claudeCode.notDetectedHint: Pick an install option below. After a local install you usually only need Check again—no app restart.
claudeCode.installLocalDesc: Uses the Synapse-bundled build—usually offline-friendly; writes under your workspace and updates PATH.
claudeCode.installWingetDesc: Install the official package via Microsoft winget (network required; admin may be required).
claudeCode.installScriptDesc: Run the official curl | bash installer (network required; macOS / Linux).
claudeCode.installSuccessHint: We check Claude / OpenCode under the Synapse workspace and bin/*.cmd shims, and merge PATH from the registry; Cursor relies on your own installation. If something is still missing, open a new terminal and check again, or restart the app.
claudeCode.installStillMissing: Install finished but no CLI was detected. Open a new terminal and check again, or restart this app.
claudeCode.needInstall: Install and detect at least one of: Claude Code, Cursor CLI, or OpenCode
claudeCode.userConfigDesc: How it works: this does not “filter” or partially merge config fields. It either deploys from the bundled template (full copy) or skips entirely when a marker file already exists (no overwrite). Claude Code: only if ~/.claude/settings.json is missing, the bundled template is copied to your home and the token is written (marketplace paths are rewritten for this machine). If that file already exists, this step does not change Claude config. OpenCode: only if ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json is missing, the bundled file is written and apiKey is filled. If it already exists, it is not overwritten.
claudeCode.companyTokenPlaceholder: Paste your company token (shared by Claude Code and OpenCode)
claudeCode.companyTokenHint: Used for Claude (ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN in settings.json) and OpenCode (apiKey in opencode.json). Do not share it.
claudeCode.llmTokenVideoLabel: Watch how to obtain the LLM token
claudeCode.userConfigLineClaudeSkipped: Claude: Config already present — skipped this run
claudeCode.userConfigLineOpencodeSkipped: OpenCode: Config already present — skipped this run
claudeCode.videoNotFound: Built-in guide video (llmtoken.mp4) not found. Ask your admin to include the video resource in the installer.

adv.apiHostWarn:
After enabling external access, any device that can reach this IP will see the web login page. Please make sure:

1. You have set a strong password (in the 'Web Access Password' section below)
2. You understand the firewall and network security risks

Are you sure you want to enable this?
adv.trustProxyHelp: Must be enabled when deployed behind a reverse proxy. Reads X-Forwarded-For for real IP and disables local password bypass.

adv.factoryResetConfirmDesc: This will permanently delete the following data with no recovery:
adv.factoryResetItem4: CLI config and process lock files

identity.title: Identity
identity.noFileSelected: Select a file from the left panel to edit
identity.fullTextInject: Full-text injection, changes take effect immediately
identity.needsCompile: Changes require compilation to take effect
identity.pageBanner: Identity files define the Agent's core personality and behavior. Improper changes may cause the Agent to behave abnormally, lose critical capabilities, or produce unexpected responses. If unsure about the impact, back up first.
identity.soulWarning: SOUL defines the Agent's core values and personality foundation. It is injected as full text into the system prompt and changes take effect immediately. Removing key principles may cause the Agent to lose honesty calibration and safety boundary constraints.
identity.agentWarning: AGENT defines task execution flow, tool usage rules, and prohibited behaviors. Improper changes may cause the Agent to fail tasks, enter infinite loops, or bypass safety restrictions. Click 'LM Optimize' to compile after editing.
identity.memoryWarning: This file is auto-refreshed daily by the system. Manual changes may be completely overwritten on the next refresh. For persistent rules, use the memory system's add_memory (type=rule).
identity.userWarning: This file is auto-updated by the learning system. Lifecycle refresh will overwrite the entire file. Removing **field**: value format lines will disable auto-learning for that field.
identity.policiesYamlWarning: This file controls tool permissions and security policies. Incorrect configuration may cause the Agent to lose access to critical tools, lose confirmation protection for dangerous operations, or disable path/command blocklists.
identity.policiesMdWarning: When merged with system policies, sections with the same '## title' will be overridden by the system version. Only non-system sections are editable.
identity.runtimeWarning: This is a compiled artifact that the Agent actually loads. Manual edits can fine-tune behavior but will be overwritten on recompilation.
identity.confirmRiskSoulAgent: This change will directly affect every conversation the Agent has with all users.
identity.tokenOverBudget: Exceeds token budget, will be truncated when injected into system prompt
identity.compiledOverwriteWarning: Recompilation will overwrite your manual edits to compiled artifacts. Continue?
identity.charCountExceeded: Exceeds character limit, will be auto-truncated on save
identity.validationErrors: Validation failed, please fix and retry

feishu.qrSubtitle: Scan with Feishu/Lark app to create a bot application
feishu.qrScanHint: Scan the QR code above with Feishu/Lark app
feishu.qrTimeout: QR code timed out, please retry
feishu.groupModeHint_always: Reply to all messages in group (use with caution)
feishu.groupModeHint: Requires enabling the sensitive permission "Get all group messages" (im:message.group_msg) under App → Bot → Permissions, otherwise the bot only receives @mentions

security.title: Security Policy
security.desc: Manage agent security isolation and sandbox configuration. Six-layer protection system prevents accidental damage.
security.zonesDesc: Configure paths for four security zones. Unmatched paths default to Protected (read-only).
security.commandsDesc: Configure dangerous command pattern matching. CRITICAL patterns are auto-denied, HIGH patterns require confirmation and run in sandbox.
security.sandboxDesc: Configure OS-level sandbox execution, only enabled for HIGH-risk commands. Normal operations are unaffected.
security.confirmationDesc: Configure security confirmation behavior, timeout, and cache policy.
security.modeCautiousDesc: Both MEDIUM and HIGH risk operations require confirmation
security.modeSmartDesc: MEDIUM auto-allowed after repeated approvals; HIGH always confirms
security.modeYoloDesc: Only CRITICAL blocked; everything else auto-allowed
security.confirmTtlDesc: Identical operations won't prompt again within this period
security.allowlistDesc: Rules added via 'Allow Always'. Persist across restarts.
security.selfProtectionDesc: Configure agent self-protection to prevent accidental damage to critical directories.
security.deathSwitchMultiplierDesc: Death switch also triggers when total denials = threshold × multiplier
security.readonlyModeActive: Agent is in read-only mode (death switch triggered)
security.sandboxEnabledDesc: Enable or disable command execution sandbox
security.sandboxBackendDesc: Select the backend technology for isolated execution
security.sandboxRiskLevelsDesc: Commands at selected risk levels will run in sandbox

agentTab.sampleKbBody: See docs/engineering/finance/precision-loss.md — floating-point risk; use BigDecimal with RoundingMode.
agentTab.javaSpecBody: No Double/Float for money; DTOs and entities must use decimal types.

agentTab.riskBody: Floating-point discount math can cause cent-level drift.
